title: Odd Lots: Inside Hudson River Trading’s Token Burn (Podcast)
summary: Today’s episode, which was recorded at our recent live show at New York’s City Winery, follows up on a conversation we had with Iain Dunning, head of AI at Hudson River Trading. Last year, we talked about how his firm uses AI. Now, some seven months later, we follow up on how one of the biggest market makers around is deploying this technology. title: Macquarie Chair Stevens to Helm $89 Billion Australia State Fund
summary: Macquarie Group Ltd. chair and former governor of the Reserve Bank of Australia Glenn Stevens will become the next chair of the New South Wales Treasury Corporation, a finance and investment arm of the Australian state that manages around A$125 billion ($89 billion).

summary: Xavier Becerra, a former congressman and US health secretary in the Biden administration, is projected to advance in the California governor’s race, one of the most expensive state elections in US history.

title: Happy Funeral in Silicon Valley: How Billionaires Struggled to Sway California Voters
summary: The most expensive gubernatorial contest in the US showed the limits to billionaires’ influence

title: Wall Street Sees SpaceX’s AI Revenue 100 Times Higher by 2030
summary: Research analysts across Wall Street are telling would-be SpaceX IPO buyers that they’re modeling for the company’s artificial intelligence division to see 100 times revenue growth at the end of the decade, to help justify a targeted $1.8 trillion valuation.

title: New ‘60 Minutes’ Chief Tells Staff Ellisons Won’t Influence Show
summary: Nick Bilton, the new executive producer of 60 Minutes, sought to quell newsroom unrest after former anchor Scott Pelley accused the program of bending to political pressure.

title: ‘Sell Indonesia’ Sweeps Trading Desks as Prabowo Tightens Grip
summary: Global investors are rapidly losing confidence in Indonesia as the nation’s stocks tumble at the fastest pace worldwide and its currency sinks to all-time lows.

title: S&P 500 Weekly Streak at Risk as AI Slide Deepens: Markets Wrap
summary: The S&P 500 is set to break a historic weekly run of gains as the artificial-intelligence trade takes another leg lower, with investors also expecting payrolls data to affirm that interest rates will stay higher for longer.

title: Danantara Plans Low-Yield Bond as Investors Shun Indonesia
summary: Indonesia’s sovereign wealth fund Danantara is planning to sell local-currency bonds that will pay interest below market rates at a time when global investors are rapidly losing confidence in the nation’s markets.

title: Southeast Asia Gets Some Inflation Relief as Fuel Pressures Ease
summary: The war-driven inflation surge unexpectedly eased in two Southeast Asian nations, signaling central banks in the region may have a bit more breathing room before needing to raise interest rates.

title: Gold Declines as Uncertainty Surrounds Progress in US-Iran Talks
summary: Gold headed for a weekly loss as uncertainty persisted over progress in US-Iran talks to end the war that’s roiled global markets.

title: AllianzGI Is Said in Exclusive Talks to Buy UOB Asset Management
summary: Allianz Global Investors is in exclusive talks to buy the asset management division of Singapore’s United Overseas Bank Ltd., according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Purbaya Slams ‘Sell Indonesia’ Trade, Won’t Quit as Finance Head
summary: Indonesian Finance Minister Purbaya Yudhi Sadewa defended his economic track record and said he’d stay in his job after the rupiah plunged to a record low, pointing to inflows into the debt market as proof investors remain optimistic.

title: Schonfeld Sees Possible Yield Spike If Burnham Wins
summary: Colin Lancaster, EMEA Head and Global Co-Head of Discretionary Macro and Fixed Income at Schonfeld, discusses the gilt market and how the upcoming Makerfield by-election may deliver further volatility. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Carmignac Sees Deficits Making Central Banks Inflation Tolerant
summary: Carmignac is extending the maturity of its inflation-linked bond holdings in a bet that mounting pressure on government budgets will push central banks to tolerate higher inflation in coming years.

title: Global Food Prices Steady Near Highest Level in Three Years
summary: Global food prices remained near the highest level in more than three years, after a drop in the cost of palm and soy oils countered disruptions to key agricultural input flows from the Iran war.

title: Korean Markets Buckle as Foreigners Sell $10 Billion in a Week
summary: After drawing much awe with the pace of its rise this year, South Korea’s $4.7 trillion stock market is starting to flash signs of strain.

title: China Allows Some Banks to Offer Higher Rates on Dollar Deposits
summary: China is allowing some banks to offer higher interest rates on corporate US dollar deposits, according to people familiar with the matter, a move that may damp the yuan’s recent gains by encouraging companies to slow their currency conversion.

title: Indonesia Releases Details on Export Overhaul of Palm, Coal
summary: Indonesia unveiled a much-anticipated list of commodities subject to its new export regime, showing the rules will capture most of its major palm oil products in addition to coal and ferronickel.

title: Jollibee Is Said to Mull Moving US Listing to Booming Hong Kong
summary: Jollibee Foods Corp. is considering switching a potential spinoff and listing of its international business from the US to Hong Kong, where share sales are flying, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Once a Turkey Bull, Algebris Shifts to CDS as Credit Risks Rise
summary: Algebris Investments is buying default protection on Turkish bonds, as it sees a higher probability of a credit event with the Iran war increasing strains on the economy.

title: EM Stocks, Currencies Dragged Down by Korean Market Selloff
summary: Emerging market assets fell for the third straight session, led by a selloff in South Korean equities as investor enthusiasm for artificial intelligence stocks waned.

title: Bank of Ghana Says Economy Remains Resilient
summary: Ghana's central bank says there could be room to resume monetary easing if the impact of external shocks begins to fade. Governor Johnson Pandit Asiama told Bloomberg's Jennifer Zabasajja that the country remains resilient for now, despite higher oil prices and ongoing uncertainty linked to the Iran conflict. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: India Cuts Bond Tax, Eases Access for Global Funds to Lift Rupee
summary: India’s central bank and government took coordinated steps to shore up the rupee after it plunged to a record low, turning to its playbook from the 2013 taper tantrum to spur foreign inflows.

title: China Crude Buying Seen Languishing for Months as Demand Tumbles
summary: China’s crude imports plunged in May to the lowest level in a decade and could languish for the coming months, as weaker demand, refinery cuts and limited exports help the world’s top buyer weather the impact of the Iran War.

title: Ghana Central Bank Head Sees Scope for Rate Cuts After Iran War
summary: Bank of Ghana Governor Johnson Asiama played down the weakness of the cedi this year and said policymakers could resume cutting interest rates once the Iran war ends.

title: Hidden AI Winners in India Add $47 Billion on Data-Center Frenzy
summary: The overarching narrative is that Indian equity markets missed out on the global artificial intelligence boom. But a look under the hood reveals a slew of smaller firms winning from trillions of dollars being spent on AI capacity.

title: China Scrutiny of Cross-Border Flows Sparks Concerns in HK | The China Show 6/5/2026
summary: “Bloomberg: The China Show” is your definitive source for news and analysis on the world's second-biggest economy. From politics and policy to tech and trends, David Ingles and Yvonne Man give global investors unique insight, delivering in-depth discussions with the newsmakers who matter. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Horizons Middle East & Africa 6/6/2026
summary: Horizons Middle East & Africa is your daily spotlight on one of the world's fastest-growing regions. Live from Dubai, we bring you the latest global markets and analysis, plus news-making interviews, with a special focus on MEA. All that and more, as you head to the office in the Gulf, pause for lunch in Hong Kong, or start your day in London or Johannesburg.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Oil Steadies After First Drop This Week on Peace Talk Optimism
summary: Oil steadied as optimism over US-Iran peace talks countered uncertainty surrounding a ceasefire deal between Israel and Lebanon.

title: Fahmy: Trump Weary of Returning to Obama Era JCPOA
summary:
There has been no sign of progress in ceasefire talks between the US and Iran after the worst burst of violence in weeks and as the Tehran-backed Hezbollah rejected a US-brokered truce in Lebanon. Dalia Fahmy, Director of International Relations and Diplomacy and Associate Professor of Political Science at Long Island University spoke to Bloomberg’s Abeer Abu Omar on Horizons Middle East and Africa on the potential end to the war in the region.

 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: European Gas Set for Weekly Gain With US-Iran Deal Still Elusive
summary: European natural gas prices headed for a weekly gain as uncertainty over the prospects for a peace deal between the US and Iran dominated sentiment after fighting in the Middle East intensified.

title: Chinese Firm Seeks $2.6 Billion Loan for Hong Kong Data Center
summary: Chinese data center operator Range Intelligent Computing Technology Group Co. is seeking a loan of around HK$20 billion ($2.6 billion) to fund what would be Hong Kong’s largest computing facility, people familiar with the matter said.

title: Japan Spent Billions to Prop Up the Yen. Why Is It Still Weak?
summary: The yen’s weakness has become a growing issue for Japan’s policymakers, given its impact on import prices and household costs.
